sections.paragraph: , עָרֹם plur. עֲרוּמִּים, f. עֲרֻמָּה, adj. naked, Job 1:21. But naked is also used for-(a) ragged, badly clad, Job 22:6 24:7 , 10 Job 24:10 Isa 58:7 comp. Gr. γυμνός, Jas 2:15 and as to the Lat. nudus Seneca, De Benef., 5:13; Arabic مسلوخ stripped, ill-clad.-(b) used of one who, having taken off his mantle, goes only clad in his tunic (כְּתֹנֶת), 1Sa 19:24 Isa 20:2. Compare Joh 21:7 Virg. Georg. I. 229, and the note of J. H. Voss. Aurel. Vict. cap. 17. Root עָרַם No. I. 1.
